notes:
VERIFIED INDEPENDENTLY (adversarial re-fetch).

STRUCTURE: 10 cards/pack; 36-pack booster box; ETB = 8 packs. All independently re-confirmed: Amazon ASIN B07B6KMSSZ ('36 Booster Packs'), ToyWiz ('Booster Box 36 Packs'), and Walmart Business ('Contains 36 packs') corroborate displayPacks=36. pokemon.com ETB gallery + ToyWiz ETB ('8 booster packs, each containing 10 cards and 1 basic Energy') corroborate etbPacks=8 and the 4C/3U/1RH/1rare-or-better/1energy SM slot layout. Project snapshot (data/snapshot/snapshot.json -> sets['forbidden-light']) carries BOTH a 'display' (booster box) and 'etb' sealed product, so a standard sealed box genuinely exists -> shipEv=true is justified.

AVAILABLE-BUCKET CHECK (re-derived from engine data, NOT taken on faith): the snapshot's 146 FLI cards bucket EXACTLY to common 33, uncommon 36, rare 41, ultra-rare 21, secret-rare 15 -- identical to the task's stated available buckets. All distribution keys used (common, uncommon, rare, ultra-rare, secret-rare) are a strict subset. No 'shiny-rare' present (correct: FLI is NOT a special Shiny-Vault set like Hidden Fates) -- confirmed it must NOT be added. 146 total / 15 secret rares also matches Amazon + pokemon.com listings.

RARE-OR-BETTER DISTRIBUTION -- source re-fetched & math re-checked: Flipside Gaming 'A Review of Rarity in Ultra Prism' confirmed verbatim: '20 booster boxes X 36 packs equals a sample size of 720 packs'; per-box averages Standard GX 2.95, Ultra rares 1.55, Secret rares 0.65, Prism rares 3.15. Ultra Prism is the SM set immediately preceding FLI with identical pack structure.

Coarse-bucket mapping by SUMMING over 36 rare-or-better slots/box:
- ultra-rare = P(GX)+P(full-art) = (2.95+1.55)/36 = 4.50/36 = 0.1250 (exact)
- secret-rare = 0.65/36 = 0.0180556 -> 0.0181
- rare = (36-2.95-1.55-0.65)/36 = 30.85/36 = 0.8569444 -> 0.8569
Sum = 0.8569+0.1250+0.0181 = 1.0000 exactly. No-double-count check PASSED: the 5.15 value hits + 30.85 plain rares = 36 = exactly one rare-or-better per pack, the correct SM assumption.

PRISM STAR EXCLUSION re-verified at Bleeding Cool FLI Prism Star article ('it can be pulled in the reverse holo slot'). Prism Stars (3.15/box) ride the reverse-holo slot, which the engine treats as non-value filler, so they are correctly excluded from the rare slot AND from EV. Not double-allocated.

HIT-ECONOMY SANITY: 4.5 ultra-rares/box (~1 per 8 packs) and 0.65 secret/box (~1 per 1.5 boxes) sit inside era-average ranges from Flipside Part Two (GX ~1:9 packs, ultra 1:18-36, secret ~1:2 boxes), re-fetched and confirmed. No absurd 'GX every pack' claim.

CONFIDENCE = medium (honest): real, large (720-pack) PUBLISHED count keeps it above 'low', but it is the ADJACENT set (Ultra Prism) applied to FLI without a per-set FLI study, keeping it below 'high'.

SOURCE LIST CHANGES vs draft: removed the two citations whose load-bearing facts could not be confirmed via fetch -- the TCGplayer #164296 box page (anti-bot, returned no body) and replaced its role; kept the Amazon listing but added ToyWiz + Walmart as the actually-fetchable corroboration for the 36-pack fact. Common/uncommon/reverse-holo slots carry no value-bearing rarity and do not drive EV; the rare-or-better slot drives EV.
